# CANDIDATE: VWAP_OVERNIGHT_GRINDER_PARAM_SWEEP_TOP

**Filed:** 2026-06-18  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** parameter_optimization  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

The VWAP overnight grinder strategy seeks to capture mean-reversion opportunities in the overnight session using VWAP reclaim/rejection signals. The top-performing parameter combination aims to improve edge capture by tightening entry conditions (via volume multiplier and proximity filters) while maintaining favorable exit structure. However, the extremely low edge_capture (40.01 vs. OP-16 floor of 771) suggests the strategy misses two of three J winner days and only marginally profits on one winner and one loser day, indicating likely overfit to specific non-representative sessions.

## Mechanism

**Entry:** VWAP reclaim signal triggered when:  
- Current bar closes above VWAP  
- Prior bar closed below VWAP  
- Volume > vol_mult (1.5) × 20-bar average volume  
- Price proximity to VWAP < proximity_dollars ($0.15)  
- Bar body size > body_min_cents ($0.05)  
- Lookback confirmation over lookback_bars (2) bars  
- Ribbon agreement required (EMA bearish) with minimum spread ≥ ribbon_min_spread_cents (30 cents)  

**Exit:**  
- Premium stop: premium_stop_pct (-14%)  
- TP1: tp1_premium_pct (50%) of premium  
- Runner target: runner_target_pct (200%) of premium  
- Profit-lock arms at profit_lock_threshold_pct (10%) with trail offset profit_lock_stop_offset_pct (5%)  
- Quantity: qty (3) contracts with tp1_qty_fraction (0.667) sold at TP1  
- Strike: strike_offset (2) OTM put  

## Expected impact on OP-16 anchors

| J day | Current engine behavior | Proposed behavior | Delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| 4/29 winner | +$342 (baseline take) | 0 | -$342 |
| 5/01 winner | +$470 (baseline take) | +$40.01 | -$429.99 |
| 5/04 winner | +$730 (baseline take) | 0 | -$730 |
| 5/05 loser | 0 (baseline skip) | +$40.54 | +$40.54 |
| 5/06 loser | 0 (baseline skip) | 0 | 0 |
| 5/07 loser 1 | 0 (baseline skip) | 0 | 0 |
| 5/07 loser 2 | 0 (baseline skip) | 0 | 0 |

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=3 requires $1,000–$2,000 account per risk-rules.md; $1K paper account realizes ~40% of headline P&L due to 50% per-trade risk cap.
2. **Sample bias:** Entire 16-month dataset used for in-sample sweep (no OOS split); high overfit risk given low edge_capture and concentration on two non-representative days (5/01, 5/05).
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (no walk-forward test performed)
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (no realistic OPRA fill simulation conducted)
5. **Failure modes:**  
   - Worst day: Large loss possible on volatile days if VWAP signals fail (e.g., 5/04 missed winner suggests strategy avoids strong trends)  
   - Max drawdown: Unknown without full equity curve; likely significant given low win rate on J days  
   - Blow-up scenario: Consecutive losing trades in choppy markets could exceed 50% daily risk cap  
6. **Concentration:** Wide P&L ($587.72) derived from 12 trades; top 2 days (5/01, 5/05) contribute ~13.8% of wide P&L but 100% of edge_capture; requires full daily P&L breakdown for accurate concentration metric.

## Pre-merge gate

<what tests need to pass: gym validators, walk-forward, real-fills>
- Pass gym v41+ validators (no regressions in core logic)  
- Demonstrate OOS walk-forward edge_capture > 200 (25% of J floor) with Sharpe ratio > 1.0  
- Validate real-fills on top 3 J days shows < ±25% deviation from BS sim  
- Confirm concentration metric (top5_pct) < 150% in OOS window  

## Confidence

2 / 10 -- Extremely low edge_capture (40.01) indicates minimal edge; strategy misses 67% of J winner days and only marginally profits on one loser day. High likelihood of overfit to noise in 5/01 and 5/05 sessions without OOS validation.

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

Does not conflict with current leaderboard (all candidates have edge_capture ≥ 771). This candidate would be rejected at the door per OP-16 gate. Complements no existing candidates as it represents a distinct (VWAP-based) approach with negligible J-edge contribution. Not suitable for leaderboard consideration without substantial OOS improvement.
